MEDIATION ORDER TO SHOW CAUSE OF FEB-09-2011 CONTINUED TO MAR-23-2011 AT 10:00 AM IN DEPARTMENT 610. NOTICE SENT BY COURT.

LAW AND MOTION 302, DEFENDANTS K. IRA P. MCEVOY AND DOE 1, IDENTIFIED AS THE "DEBRA L. WETHERBY AND KAREN IRA POLCYN MCEVOY, CO-TRUSTEES OF THE TRUST UNDER AGREEMENT DATED MARCH 6, 2007" MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT. ARGUED AND THE COURT TOOK THE MATTER UNDER SUBMISSION. COURT RULED ON PLAINTIFFS OBJECTIONS TO EVIDENCE, OVERRULED IN PART & SUSTAINED IN PART. ORDER ON OBJECTIONS SIGNED. --- AFTER HEARING THE COURT RULED AS FOLLOWS: TENTATIVE RULING ADOPTED AS THE FINAL RULING - DEFENDANTS' MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT IS DENIED. AS TO DEFENDANTS' ARGUMENT THAT PLAINTIFFS' COMPLAINT IS BARRED BY THE WORKER'S COMPENSATION LAWS, DEFENDANT HAS SUBMITTED EVIDENCE TENDING TO SHOW PLAINTIFF WAS MORE LIKELY AN EMPLOYEE AND SUCCESSFULLY SHIFTED THE BURDEN TO PLAINTIFF. HOWEVER, PLAINTIFF HAS PRESENTING CONFLICTING EVIDENCE SUFFICIENT TO RAISE TRIABLE ISSUES OF MATERIAL FACT REGARDING APPLICATION OF THE BORELLO FACTORS AND WHETHER PLAINTIFF WAS AN EMPLOYEE OR INDEPENDENT CONTRACTOR. AS TO THE "VETERINARIAN RULE" DEFENSE, PLAINTIFF HAS PRESENTED SUFFICIENT EVIDENCE TO RAISE A TRIABLE ISSUE OF FACT REGARDING THE EXCEPTION TO THE "VETERINARIAN RULE" THAT DEFENDANT WAS AWARE OF SCOOBY'S VICIOUS PROPENSITIES. PLAINTIFF TO PREPARE AN ORDER REFLECTING THE COURT'S RULING. JUDGE: LORETTA M. GIORGI, REPORTER: KARLA ELLIS-DAVIS, CSR #12998

MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT
Off Calendar May-04-2011 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
DEFENDANTS K. IRA P. MCEVOY AND DOE 1, IDENTIFIED AS THE "DEBRA L. WETHERBY AND KAREN IRA POLCYN MCEVOY, CO-TRUSTEES OF THE TRUST UNDER AGREEMENT DATED MARCH 6, 2007" MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT.

APPEARANCES: Andrew Rhine, Esq. of Critchlow Diskint Rhine & Pirelli 888/873-0952 for pltf & opposing party; Karl J. F. Runft, Esq. & Richard S. Diestel, Esq. of Bledsoe, Cathcart, Diestel, Pedersen & Treppa LLP 951-2807 for deft & moving party.

RULING: Argued and the Court took the matter under submission.
Court ruled on plaintiffs objections to evidence, overruled in part & sustained in part. Order on objections signed.
After hearing the Court ruled as follows: Tentative ruling adopted as the final ruling - DEFENDANTS' MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT IS DENIED. AS TO DEFENDANTS' ARGUMENT THAT PLAINTIFFS' COMPLAINT IS BARRED BY THE WORKER'S COMPENSATION LAWS, DEFENDANT HAS SUBMITTED EVIDENCE TENDING TO SHOW PLAINTIFF WAS MORE LIKELY AN EMPLOYEE AND SUCCESSFULLY SHIFTED THE BURDEN TO PLAINTIFF. HOWEVER, PLAINTIFF HAS PRESENTING CONFLICTING EVIDENCE SUFFICIENT TO RAISE TRIABLE ISSUES OF MATERIAL FACT REGARDING APPLICATION OF THE BORELLO FACTORS AND WHETHER PLAINTIFF WAS AN EMPLOYEE OR INDEPENDENT CONTRACTOR. AS TO THE "VETERINARIAN RULE" DEFENSE, PLAINTIFF HAS PRESENTED SUFFICIENT EVIDENCE TO RAISE A TRIABLE ISSUE OF FACT REGARDING THE EXCEPTION TO THE "VETERINARIAN RULE" THAT DEFENDANT WAS AWARE OF SCOOBY'S VICIOUS PROPENSITIES.
Plaintiff to prepare an order reflecting the Court's ruling.
(302/LMG) Judge: Loretta M. Giorgi, Reporter: Karla Ellis-Davis, CSR #12998
